question: Answer the questions after reading fig. a) What type of graph is shown here? b) What does the graph show? c) Which country’s tourism has a larger share in the contribution towards GDP?
answer:
The type of graph shown is a multiple or joint bar graph.
The graph shows the percentage of the population engaged in tourism and its contribution to GDP in Brazil and India, respectively.
The contribution towards GDP of Brazil is more than that of India. In Brazil, nearly 8% of the population is engaged in the tourism industry. But their contribution to GDP is nearly 10%.
In India, the population engaged in the tourism industry is much greater than the contribution to GDP. Nearly 10% of the population is engaged in the tourism industry, but their contribution is only 8%.
subject: Geography
chapter: Geography [English] Standard 10 Tourism, Transport and Communication

question: What are the factors responsible for development of these tourism sites in Brazil?
answer:
Tourism is one of the fastest-growing service industries in Brazil. Tourism rates have been rising steeply in Brazil since the 2000s. There was a setback in the tourism industry between 2006 and 2008, but the economy has been back on track since then. Today, Brazil is one of the most visited countries in South America.
Brazil’s cultural integrity and natural beauty are not the only factors that draw tourists. Brazil is also a very popular business destination. Many MNCs have their headquarters in Brazil. The Amazon River basin and the impenetrable forests, wide beaches and bays on the coast, attractive islands, and the innumerable species of flora and fauna make Brazil an attractive tourist destination. Many places are tagged by UNESCO as World Heritage Sites. Besides the capital city of Brasilia, cities like Rio de Janeiro and São Paulo also attract vast tourist traffic. Because of the country's abundant natural resources, ecotourism is also being promoted with the participation of local people.
Most of the visitors are from Argentina, the United States and Italy. To promote tourism, tourist VISA requirements have been renounced for many countries, including Greece, Italy, Hong Kong, Poland, Turkey and many others. Thus, tourism is a large developing industry in Brazil.
subject: Geography
chapter: Geography [English] Standard 10 Tourism, Transport and Communication

question: Study the transport map of Brazil in the figure and answer the following questions: 1. Which means of transport are seen on the map? 2. Which means of transport has a denser network?
answer:
ll the means of transport, like roadways, railways, airways, and waterways, are seen on the map.
The roadways have a denser network.
The Trans Amazonian Highway can be seen prominently. It connects Cruzeiro do Sul, Porto Velho, Manaus, and Brasília.
South-east Brazil is the richest and most populous region of the country. Agriculture, mining, and tourism are well developed in this part of Brazil.
The reasons for the development of railways in the South-East are:

Brazil’s natural environment, including the Amazon forest in the north and swampy plains, is not conducive to agriculture. Development of railways. This region has a minimal population.
South-east Brazil’s fertile and accessible terrain allows for train links. This location is ideal for trade and business because of its proximity to popular tourist destinations, like Brasilia and Rio de Janeiro.
subject: Geography
chapter: Geography [English] Standard 10 Tourism, Transport and Communication

question: Considering the development of transport in a place, which factors do you think are responsible for the development of transport in Brazil?
answer:
The transport system is the service by which persons and goods move from one place to another using humans, animals or vehicles. The transport system establishes the links between producing centres and consuming centres. Transport provides the link through which trade takes place. The transport system also acts as an artery for communication within the nation and among nations. The principal modes of transportation are landways, waterways, airways and through pipelines. To be established as a major trading centre, the country must have a well-connected transit network. The integrated operation of all transportation means is essential to their effective functioning. Thus the development of transport is very important for a country’s progress.
The most common method of transportation in Brazil is the roadways. Most of the country’s transportation requirements are fulfilled by the roadways. But the density of the road network is higher in the eastern part. Brazil has the potential to be the country using waterways as an important means of transportation. Sea transportation is mostly used in Brazil and inland river transportation is not highly utilised. The inland river transportation network is only 13% of the total transportation network in Brazil. The waterways are not highly developed, especially in the interior regions, because of the occurrence of many waterfalls and the difficulty in navigation in these areas with strong currents. Also, these areas are far from the other major centres, making their development highly uneconomical.
Rail transport started in Brazil from the 19th century. Still, the most common means of transportation in Brazil is the roadways or waterways. The extent of railways in much smaller in Brazil. It is nearly five times smaller than the extent of roadways in Brazil. The density of the rail network is concentrated in the eastern part of the country. The internal region is actually devoid of the rail network. There are many difficulties in the development of the rail network in the internal areas. The dense, impenetrable Amazon forests and the vast tributaries that form the Amazon basin are the main reasons for the lack of a rail network in the internal regions compared to the eastern side of the country. Some areas may be defined by certain tribal groups. The acquisition of land for the commencement of the rail network is also a big issue. The use of the rail network for transportation is highly restricted in the country.

Thus, the development of roadways and waterways is the main modes of transportation that could be used in Brazil given its topography and drainage.
subject: Geography
chapter: Geography [English] Standard 10 Tourism, Transport and Communication

question: Look at the transport maps of India given in the figures and answer the following questions. 1. Which means of transport are visible on the map?
answer:
The means of transport visible on the map are waterways, railways and airways.
The density of railways is higher in the Northern Plains region.
Ports: Mumbai, Vishakhapatanam, Haldia, Chennai and Kandla. 
Airports: Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru, Chennai, and Kolkata.
The north and the north-east parts of India have a sparse network of transport. Due to mountainous and snow-covered areas in the north and the dense inaccessible forest areas of the north-east, the transportation network is sparse here. Also, these regions have low population and a lack of economic opportunities.
subject: Geography
chapter: Geography [English] Standard 10 Tourism, Transport and Communication

question: Arun called his mother from Digboi at 7 am. At what local time will his mother pick up his call at Jaisalmer?
answer:
Both Digboi (Assam) and Jaisalmer (Rajasthan) follow the same time zone, Indian Standard Time (IST).
Although Digboi is located much farther east than Jaisalmer and experiences sunrise earlier, India uses a single standard time throughout the country.
Therefore, if Arun called his mother from Digboi at 7:00 a.m., his mother in Jaisalmer would receive the call at 7:00 a.m. IST.
subject: Geography
chapter: Geography [English] Standard 10 Tourism, Transport and Communication

question: Read the map in the figure and answer the following questions: 1. How many divisions can you see in the map? 2. What do these divisions signify? 3. What does the term ‘behind the GMT’ mean?
answer:
There are four divisions in the map.
These divisions signify the time zones in Brazil.
The Greenwich Meridian Time is a time set with reference to 0° longitude that passes through Greenwich. The countries lying to the west of 0° longitude are ‘behind the GMT’.
The easternmost part of Brazil is ahead of the others.
This part is the easternmost point and is 60-168 minutes ahead of the others.
The red line on the map is the official BRT, which is in the GMT-03 time zone. It is 3 hours behind GMT.
subject: Geography

question: Study the map given in figure and answer the following questions. 1. Calculate the difference between the two longitudinal extremes of mainland India. Is it more than Brazil?
answer:
The difference between the two longitudinal extremes of east and west mainland India is about 29°, whereas the difference between the two longitudinal extremes of east and west mainland Brazil is about 39°. No, it is not more than Brazil. 
The 82° 30′ E longitude is called Indian Standard Time (IST). 
The IST time is 5 hours and 30 minutes ahead of the GMT.
One of the 29 local times (82° 30' E Longitude) is considered IST in India.
subject: Geography
chapter: Geography [English] Standard 10 Tourism, Transport and Communication

question: It is 12 noon at Delhi. What would be the local time in Brasilia?
answer: It is 12 noon in Delhi, but according to Greenwich time, it is 6.30 a.m. in Greenwich, and in Brasilia, it would be 3.30 in the afternoon.
subject: Geography

question: Saurabh and Ashwini work for a MNC. Two of their regional head offices are located in Brazil in Rio De Janeiro and Manaus, respectively. Both of them have to contact either of the head offices
answer:
1. Let’s assume Saurabh is coordinating with Rio De Janeiro’s head office, and Ashwini is coordinating with the head office of Manaus. 
2. Brazil has four time zones. It is behind the GMT by 2, 3, 4 or 5 hours. 
3. Rio De Janeiro is in the 2nd time zone, which is 3 hours behind GMT (GMT-03), and Manaus is in the 3rd time zone, which is 4 hours behind GMT (GMT-04).
4. Now, the time difference between GMT and IST is 5 hrs. 30 mins.
5. They have to make the following adjustments:

Saurabh

Rio De Janeiro
GMT
IST

10 a.m.
1 p.m.
6.30 p.m.

5 p.m.
8 p.m.
1.30 a.m.

 

Ashwini

Manaus
GMT
IST

10 a.m.
2 p.m.
7.30 p.m.

5 p.m.
9 p.m.
2.30 a.m.

Rio De Janeiro is in the 2nd time zone, which is 3 hours behind GMT (GMT-03), and Manaus is in the 3rd time zone, which is 4 hours behind GMT (GMT-04)
Corresponding timings in India Saurabh: 6.30 p.m. to 1.30 a.m. Ashwini: 7.30 p.m. to 2.30 a.m.

question: On what basis will you decide how many standard times should be there in a country?
answer:
Standard time of a country is the time of the country determined legally and is binding on all the regions. It is the harmonisation of different time zones in a geographical area rather than using solar time. It was established in the 19th century to enable weather forecasting and set railway timings. Some of the importance of following a standard time should be considered while deciding the number of standard times to be followed in any country:

It helps in avoiding confusion about time differences that can happen between the extreme points in the same country.
It helps in properly scheduling the railway and the airway timings.
It helps in making defined schedules for meetings with delegates from other foreign countries.
It helps to coordinate activities and reduces the loss of time incurred in adjusting the time zones.
It helps to improve communication within the same country.

question: State whether it is right or wrong with reasons. The indicator of development in a country is the development of transport in that country.
answer:
The given statement is right.
Reasons:

The development of transport is an important indicator of a country’s development because a well-developed transport system (roads, railways, airways, and waterways) helps in the movement of goods, people, and services efficiently.
Efficient transport promotes trade, industrial growth, employment opportunities, and regional connectivity, which together contribute to the overall economic and social development of a country.

question: State whether right or wrong with reasons. Brazil’s time is ahead of India’s time.
answer:
The given statement is wrong.
Reasons:

India is 8 hours 30 minutes ahead of BRT. Brazil itself has 4 time zones, while India considers 82.5°E longitude as the Indian Standard Time.
IST is 5 hours 30 minutes ahead of Greenwich Meridian Time, while Brazilian time is 3 hours behind GMT. That makes India’s time ahead of Brazil’s time.

question: State whether right or wrong with reasons. The development of tourism in India has begun recently.
answer:
The given sentence is right.
Reasons:

India possesses great natural diversity, with extensive coastal regions and numerous historical sites.
Owing to its attractive landscapes, heritage monuments, and various other unique features, the number of foreign tourists visiting India is on the rise. Both foreign and domestic tourists travel across the country for experiences like marine tourism, wildlife tourism, medical tourism, and more. Consequently, many tourist spots have been developed with facilities such as accommodation, transportation, and food.
With the rise in tourism, the country earns foreign exchange, contributing to India’s economic growth.

This shows that tourism development in India has begun and is expanding rapidly.

answer:

There was a setback in the tourism industry between 2006 and 2008, but the economy has been back on track since then. Today, Brazil is one of the most visited countries in South America.

Brazil’s cultural integrity and natural beauty are not the only factors that draw tourists. Brazil is also a very popular business destination. Many MNCs have their headquarters in Brazil.
The Amazon River basin and the impenetrable forests, widespread beaches and bays on the coast, attractive islands, and the innumerable species of flora and fauna make Brazil an attractive tourist destination.

question: What are the difficulties in the development of the railway system in Brazil’s internal areas?
answer:
The densely forested plains of the Amazon River Basin, as well as swampy lands, prevented railway expansion in Brazil.
Large areas of Brazil are unpopulated. Roadways are the most popular means of transportation in Eastern Brazil, and waterways have developed commercially along the Amazon River.
Only a few cities, as well as visitors, use trains for long-distance transportation. These are the challenges in developing the railway system in Brazil’s internal regions.
subject: Geography
chapter: Geography [English] Standard 10 Tourism, Transport and Communication

question: Which means of communication has expedited the field of communications?
answer:
Today’s modes of communication include telephone, mobile phone, television, and the internet.
Brazil’s communication system is more advanced and effective than India’s. In Brazil, about 45% of the population uses the internet, whereas in India, mobile phones are more widely used.
India is the greatest consumer of smart mobile phones and internet services.

Thus, the expansion of the internet, the increasing number of satellites and the increasing use of mobile phones have expedited the area of communication.

question: Match the columns. ‘A’ Group (a) Trans-Amazonian (b) Road Transport (c) Rio de Janeiro (d) Manmad ‘B’ Group (i) Tourist Place Highway (ii) Railway Station in India (iii) Golden Quadrilateral
answer:
‘A’ Group
‘B’ Group

(a) Trans-Amazonian
(iv) Major Highways

(b) Road Transport
(iii) Golden Quadrilateral

(c) Rio de Janeiro
(i) Tourist Place Highway

(d) Manmad
(ii) Railway Station in India

Explanation:

Trans-Amazonian is an important highway in Brazil. It was inaugurated on 27 September 1972. It is 4000 km long. It is the third-largest highway in Brazil. It was commissioned to integrate the interior northern parts of Brazil with the rest of the country.
The golden Quadrilateral is an important national highway network of India. It connects the major centres of India and the four metros - Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, and Kolkata. It is maintained by the National Highway Authority of India. It was completed in 2012. It is the largest highway project undertaken in India.
Rio de Janeiro is an important tourist place in Brazil situated in the 40 ̊ W Meridian. Its proper coordinates are 22° 54 S, 43° 12 W. It has the second-largest economy of Brazil.
Manmad is a major railway station of the Central Railway zone. It is located in the Nasik district of Maharashtra, India. It was opened in 1886.

question: Give geographical reasons. Eco-tourism is being developed more in Brazil.
answer:
Ecotourism is the tourist activities mainly engaged towards the inherent natural environment to support preservation and conservation of the exquisite flora and fauna.
Eco-tourism means responsible travel to natural areas without causing damage to the environment.
Ecotourism is being developed more in Brazil because of its richness in natural resources. 
Brazil has the largest number of vegetation species in the world, including the Amazon, the largest rainforest in the world and a great diversity of wildlife.
The Amazon River basin and the impenetrable forests, widespread beaches and bays on the coast, attractive islands and the innumerable species of flora and fauna make Brazil an attractive tourist destination.
Because of the abundance of natural resources in the country, ecotourism is also being promoted with the participation of local people.
subject: Geography
chapter: Geography [English] Standard 10 Tourism, Transport and Communication

question: Give geographical reasons. The waterways are not developed in Brazil.
answer:
1Show Solution
The waterways in Brazil are not well developed due to geographical challenges.
Most Brazilian rivers flow at high speeds, making navigation difficult.
The rivers in Brazil are located in highlands, which makes them less accessible.
The remoteness of these rivers further limits their development and utilisation.
Many of Brazil’s rivers have a huge amount of discharge, which complicates their use for transportation.
subject: Geography
chapter: Geography [English] Standard 10 Tourism, Transport and Communication

question: Give geographical reasons. A dense network of railways has developed in the north Indian plains.
answer:
Railways are a simple and convenient means of transportation. It is useful for both personal and commercial transportation.
The railway network is dense in the North Indian plains and in areas with gentle slopes.
Also, this region has a large population and well-developed agriculture and businesses.

This has led to increased urbanisation in these regions, resulting in a dense network of railways in the north Indian plains.
subject: Geography
chapter: Geography [English] Standard 10 Tourism, Transport and Communication

question: Give geographical reasons. Development of transport is important for a country’s progress.
answer:
well-developed transport system is critical to a country’s financial, social, and cultural development.
Apart from people, products require effective transit facilities. This is critical to the nation’s progress.
Road transport can use a dense network of roadways to connect even the most isolated locations.
Also, while waterways are relatively cheap sources of transportation, they are important for doing business and trade.
Airways promote a country’s internationally connection while also promoting trade and communication.
It might be argued that the export of goods from the country and the resulting foreign currency are only conceivable because of a well-developed transportation system.

Hence, a good transport system has the potential to improve the economy and further promote the country.
subject: Geography
chapter: Geography [English] Standard 10 Tourism, Transport and Communication

question: Give geographical reasons. We rely on the sea route for international trade.
answer:
Foreign trade or international trade is the exchange of goods and services between countries. It is an important component of the economy.
Waterways have been the most common means of transport from earlier days. Waterways are the cheapest and easiest way of carrying bulky goods over long distances.
One of its greatest benefits is that it does not require any route construction or maintenance.
Sea routes and oceanic routes are most beneficial for transporting bulky goods from one country to another.
Waterways is a major means of domestic and foreign trade. But, to establish as a major trading centre, the port must have a well-connected rail and road transit network.
subject: Geography
chapter: Geography [English] Standard 10 Tourism, Transport and Communication

question: Differentiate between. Water transport in the Amazon and the Ganga river.
answer:
Sr. No.
Basis
Water transport in river Amazon
Water transport in river Ganga

1.
Nature
The Amazon is the largest river basin in the world.
The Ganga is the largest river basin in India.

2.
Types of transportation practices
Mostly, commercialised transportation is practised with some degree of traditional methods.
Mostly, primitive and traditional methods are used. The commercialised version is used only in the eastern parts.

3.
Intensity of requirement 
Water transport in the Amazon River is highly necessary only in the eastern parts with a higher population. 
Water transport in river Ganga is required in every part of the region as it is one of the most densely populated areas of the country.

4.
Degree of development
Water transport in the Amazon River is not highly developed in the interior parts due to the occurrence of waterfalls, strong currents, and impenetrable forests.
Water transport in the river Ganga is highly developed throughout the region due to the plain topography.
subject: Geography
chapter: Geography [English] Standard 10 Tourism, Transport and Communication

question: Differentiate between: Communication in Brazil and India.
answer:
Sr. No.
Communication in Brazil
Communication in India

1.
The communication system in Brazil is extremely developed and effective.
In India, due to the progress of electronic media, telecommunications is a fast-growing industry.

2.
The physical composition of Brazil, its sparsely populated regions, dense forests, etc., are major obstacles in the development of telecommunication services.
In India, the use of mobile phones and digital media is increasing. India has become one of the largest users of mobile phones and the internet.

3.
However, based on the technology developed in Brazil to date, it is now aiming to launch satellites into space.
On the basis of self-developed technology, the internet, and satellite technology, India is paving the way for the expansion of telecommunications.
subject: Geography
chapter: Geography [English] Standard 10 Tourism, Transport and Communication

question: Differentiate between: Indian standard time (IST) and Brazilian standard time (BRT).
answer:
Sr. No.
Basis
IST
BRT

1.
Time zones
India has a single time zone.
Brazil has four time zones.

2.
The difference between Greenwich time
India is in the eastern hemisphere, and Indian Standard Time is 5 hours and 30 minutes ahead of Greenwich Mean Time.
Brazil is in the Western Hemisphere, and its four time zones are 2, 3, 4, and 5 hours behind Greenwich Mean Time. BRT is 3 hours behind GMT.

3.
Difference between extreme points 
In India, the difference between the two extreme longitudinal points is 120 minutes (2 hours). India uses a single standard time across the country.
In Brazil, the difference between the two extreme longitudinal points is 168 minutes (2 hours and 48 minutes). Brazil has 4 time zones.

4.
Time difference
IST stands for Indian Standard Time.
BRT stands for Brazilian Time.
subject: Geography
chapter: Geography [English] Standard 10 Tourism, Transport and Communication

question: Write a note on modern means of communication.
answer:
Communication is the process of sending or receiving information from others through different means of communication. In the earlier days, letters and word-of-mouth were the only means of communication. Newspapers became widespread with the development of the printing press. However, the development of technology has accelerated the communication process in the world.

Internet and satellites are the harbingers of today’s fast-expanding digital communication network. Information and Communication Technology (IT) is an important driving force for the success of today’s communication.
Telecommunication facilities, especially the Internet, have significantly contributed to the development of communication between countries on different continents.
The development of services like e-banking has increased the pace at which transactions are carried out around the world. Today, the world is just a click away. Information is transmitted in seconds, and the world has shrunk inside our hands.
Any information can be accessed via the internet, and communication has become highly centralised through satellites. The information revolution through digital means like mobile phones and social media has also expanded the communication network between countries.
subject: Geography
chapter: Geography [English] Standard 10 Tourism, Transport and Communication

question: Write a note on air transport in India.
answer:
irways is the fastest mode of transport of the century. It is also the most expensive means. Aeroplanes and helicopters are the major air transport modes. 
Presently, airways are the only mode of transport to reach remote locations that have no road or rail connectivity. 
Helicopters play a major role during natural disasters or wars for rescuing people and distributing food and other essentials, especially in inaccessible areas. 
It is essential in a country like India that has different terrains and long distances between places.
Air transport in India was started in 1911. It is managed by Air India. IndiGo, Air India, SpiceJet, and GoAir are the major transit corporations.
India’s is the world’s third-largest domestic and overall civil aviation sector. India has a total of 499 airports.
To achieve connectivity and utilise the potential of under-rated airports, the government has launched the UDAN scheme in April 2017.
subject: Geography
chapter: Geography [English] Standard 10 Tourism, Transport and Communication

question: Write a note on correlation between physiographic and internal waterways.
answer:
India has an extensive network of waterways, including lakes, canals, oceans, and other inland water systems. The total navigable area is 14,500 km.
Waterways have been the most common means of transport from earlier days. Waterways are the cheapest and easiest way of carrying bulky goods over long distances.
One of its greatest benefits is that it requires no route construction or maintenance. Waterways can be inland waterways or sea routes.
Navigable rivers, canals, backwaters, and lakes are used as inland waterways. It was the major means of transportation before the advent of railways and roadways.
Inland waterways are the only means of transportation through dense, impenetrable forests and areas defined by certain tribal groups.
Sea and oceanic routes are primarily used to transport bulky goods from one country to another.
Waterways are a major means of domestic and foreign trade.
subject: Geography
chapter: Geography [English] Standard 10 Tourism, Transport and Communication

question: Write a note on importance of Standard Time.
answer:
Standard time of a country is the time of the country determined legally and is binding on all the regions. It is the harmonisation of different time zones in a geographical area rather than using solar time. It was established in the 19th century to enable weather forecasting and set railway timings. Some of the importance of following a standard time is:

It helps in avoiding confusion about time differences that can happen between the extreme points in the same country.
It helps in properly scheduling the railway and the airway timings.
It helps in making defined schedules for meetings with delegates from other foreign countries.
It helps to coordinate activities and reduces the loss of time incurred in adjusting the time zones.
It helps to improve communication within the same country.

question: Visit a harbour/airport/communication centre and write down its information.
answer:
Harbour (Mumbai Port)

Name: Mumbai Port Authority (formerly Mumbai Port Trust)
Location: Shoorji Vallabhdas Marg, Ballard Estate, Fort, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400001
Establishment: Commissioned in 1873
Purpose: Handles bulk cargo, cruise lines, and international maritime trade.
Key Infrastructure:

Docks: Three main enclosed wet docks (Prince’s, Victoria, and Indira Docks).
Terminal: Hosts the modern Ballard Pier Station cruise terminal for international travellers.
Cargo Facilities: Dedicated berths for petroleum, chemicals, and dry bulk items.

Function & Importance: Serves as India’s premier gateway for trade, driving economic growth across Western and Central India.

Airport (CSMIA Mumbai)

Name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport (CSMIA)
Location: Sahar Road, Andheri East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400099
Operated By: Adani Airport Holdings Limited
Terminals: Two active operational zones.
Terminal 1 (Santacruz): Dedicated strictly to domestic low-cost carriers.
Terminal 2 (Sahar): Iconic, X-shaped terminal handling all international flights and premium domestic routes.
Key Infrastructure

Runways: Two intersecting runways (09/27 and 14/32) handling high-density traffic.
Cargo Complex: Advanced automated facility managing massive air freight volumes.

Function & Importance: Acts as a primary global aviation hub, connecting Western India to Europe, the Middle East, and Asia.

Communication Centre Profile (Central Railway Telecom)

Name: Central Railway Telecommunication & Network Operations Centre
Location: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus (CSMT), Fort, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400001
Purpose: Manages communication, signalling, and data transfer for train operations.
Key Systems

Optical Fibre Network: Massive under-rail fibre backbone connecting stations for secure data transmission.
Control Room: Centralized system overseeing passenger wireless networks, emergency hotlines, and track sensors.
Passenger Systems: Powers real-time digital display boards, automated voice announcements, and security CCTV matrices.

Function & Importance: Ensures safe, crash-free, and punctual routing for millions of daily commuters.
subject: Geography
chapter: Geography [English] Standard 10 Tourism, Transport and Communication
